title: Analyst, Strategic Planning and Governance (Job ID: 10074711)
description:
Responsibilities

  • Transform various business strategies including but not limited to the strategies for structured finance products, collaboration between Japanese Corporate & Global Corporate coverage,  global markets and transaction banking into outcomes by delivering and managing tactics and guidelines in timely fashion

  • Improve revenue or reduce cost in any possible ways while in compliance with rules, regulations and internal guidelines

  • By embracing change, be a good team player; train/supervise junior staff with proper succession planning

  • Promote business growth strategies for Global Corporate Banking Division for Asia and to act as a key contact person for the transactions with Japanese stakeholders and senior management in Tokyo Head Office and across APAC

  • Build relationship with internal and external customers/stakeholders, and earn trust by delivering quality work

  • Liaise and collaborate with the Tokyo Head Office and Global Corporate Banking Division in Tokyo and other stakeholders within the bank across regions, HO and business units

Requirements

  • Minimum JLPT N1 Certification (or equivalent language capabilities/proficiency) is required to effectively communicate with Japanese stakeholders and senior management in Tokyo Head Office and across APAC to promote business growth and various strategies for Global Corporate Banking Division for Asia

  • Minimum three years of experience in corporate banking or investment banking

  • Minimum 1 year experience in Japanese Corporate Coverage as the role requires handling such clients and customers.

  • Excel skills to handle several spreadsheets properly (pivot table, vlookup, etc.) and Powerpoint/Word skills to create various reports

  • A team player who is able to work/collaborate well with various stakeholders in a large Tokyo-based Japanese organization with adequate communication skills and strong sense of responsibility

  • Presentation skills to effectively communicate with internal overseas stakeholders including management members of the Bank

  • Accuracy of work to complete the duties to support management and the other marketing staff

  • Understanding and willingness to work closely with the relevant Japanese Corporate primary offices and stakeholders in Japan

  • Good understanding and business experience towards/with Japanese financial institutions

We regret to inform that only shortlisted applicants will be notified

Job ID: 10074711

postedCompany.description:
MUFG Bank, Ltd. is Japan’s premier bank, with a global network spanning in more than 40 markets. Outside of Japan, the bank offers an extensive scope of commercial and investment banking products and services to businesses, governments and individuals worldwide. MUFG Bank’s parent, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group, Inc. (MUFG) is one of the world’s leading financial groups. Headquartered in Tokyo and with over 360 years of history, the Group has about 120,000 employees and offers services including commercial banking, trust banking, securities, credit cards, consumer finance, asset management, and leasing.


The Group aims to be the world’s most trusted financial group through close collaboration among our operating companies and flexibly respond to all of the financial needs of our customers, serving society, and fostering shared and sustainable growth for a better world. MUFG’s shares trade on the Tokyo, Nagoya, and New York stock exchanges.


For more information, visit https://www.mufg.jp/english
postedCompany.name: MUFG BANK, LTD. Singapore Branch

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
